description: Cross-platform evaluation suite for the skill-lifecycle-manager skill. The suite tests whether the skill can route lifecycle work correctly, preserve a shared canonical core, generate Codex and Claude Code projections, keep installable package boundaries self-contained, keep validation and trigger-optimization concerns explicit, and respect delegated research checkpoints before authoring.

assertion_definitions.stage_routing_correct: The response chooses the smallest appropriate lifecycle route for the request instead of forcing every stage. Stages are named clearly and match the task.
assertion_definitions.canonical_core_created_or_preserved: The result treats the canonical skill package as the source of truth. It creates or updates `SKILL.md`, `skill.json`, and `CHANGELOG.md` when the task is authoring-oriented, or preserves the existing canonical package when the task is audit/optimization oriented.
assertion_definitions.projection_strategy_explicit: The response explains or applies the distinction between shared core content and platform-specific packaging or metadata. It does not silently collapse Codex and Claude Code into one undifferentiated surface.
assertion_definitions.package_boundary_respected: When installability or portability matters, the response treats cross-package private script-path dependencies as packaging defects. A skill may reference another skill conceptually, but it should stay self-contained after install unless a shared runtime is explicitly shipped with it.
assertion_definitions.codex_projection_present: For tasks that require distribution, the result generates or validates a Codex projection under `.agents/skills/<name>/` and keeps the runtime-relevant package intact.
assertion_definitions.claude_projection_filtered: For tasks that require distribution, the result generates or validates a Claude Code projection under `.claude/skills/<name>/` and omits repo-only metadata such as `skill.json`, `CHANGELOG.md`, or unrelated build artifacts.
assertion_definitions.validation_executed: The response runs or names concrete validation commands appropriate to the task, such as `quick_validate.py`, `validate_projection.py`, or repo validation commands.
assertion_definitions.trigger_work_separated: When the task includes trigger optimization, the response treats description tuning as a distinct pass and does not unnecessarily rewrite the whole skill body.
assertion_definitions.delegated_research_protocol_respected: When Discover delegates to `skill-researcher`, the response treats that skill as a binding workflow with required checkpoints and separate handoff semantics rather than as informal inspiration.
assertion_definitions.bounded_scope_respected: When the user supplies a bounded source set, platform set, or candidate set, the response keeps discovery intentionally narrow instead of widening the search by default.
assertion_definitions.depth_gate_respected: The response applies the discovery-depth gate correctly: it stops to resolve Quick, Standard, or Deep only when broad discovery is required and depth is still unspecified, and it does not re-ask when depth is already provided or the comparison is intentionally bounded.
assertion_definitions.candidate_pause_respected: The response stops after candidate inventory when user confirmation is still required and clearly asks for that confirmation before deeper research continues.
assertion_definitions.no_authoring_before_confirmation: Before the candidate set is confirmed, the response does not start drafting `SKILL.md`, `skill.json`, `CHANGELOG.md`, or target-skill references as though authoring had already begun.
assertion_definitions.next_step_explicit: The response closes with a concrete next lifecycle step, especially when real-world evaluation or install/publish work remains.

cases.name: Create a new dual-platform skill from a repeated workflow
cases.description: Tests Create/Update -> Validate -> Project route selection. The skill should produce a canonical skill package plus Codex and Claude Code projections without treating the two targets as identical.
cases.prompt: Turn our repeated API deprecation review workflow into a reusable skill. It needs to work for both Codex and Claude Code, keep one canonical source of truth, create the projections, and validate the result before you stop.

stage_routing_correct.check: The route should focus on authoring, validation, and projection. Discover is optional, but the response should not default to a full ecosystem survey unless the request or context demands it.

canonical_core_created_or_preserved.check: Creates a canonical skill package with `SKILL.md`, `skill.json`, and `CHANGELOG.md` as the durable source of truth.

projection_strategy_explicit.check: Explains or demonstrates that Codex and Claude Code projections are derived from the canonical package rather than separately hand-authored.

package_boundary_respected.check: Keeps the new skill installable as a self-contained package instead of introducing runtime dependencies on another skill package's private scripts.

codex_projection_present.check: Produces or validates a `.agents/skills/<name>/` projection that keeps the runtime package intact.

claude_projection_filtered.check: Produces or validates a `.claude/skills/<name>/` projection that excludes repo-only metadata or build artifacts.

validation_executed.check: Runs or explicitly reports `quick_validate.py` and `validate_projection.py`, plus any repo-level validation if needed.

next_step_explicit.check: Ends with the next recommended lifecycle step, such as real prompt evaluation or install smoke testing.

cases.name: Optimize trigger boundaries without rewriting the whole skill
cases.description: Tests whether trigger optimization stays a separate pass. The ideal behavior is to define should-trigger, should-not-trigger, and near-miss prompts while keeping the body stable unless the evidence points to a deeper issue.
cases.prompt: Our `skill-lifecycle-manager` works, but it under-triggers on Claude Code and sometimes over-triggers on Codex. Improve the description and prove the trigger boundary is tighter, but do not do a broad rewrite unless the eval evidence says the body is actually the problem.

stage_routing_correct.check: The route should emphasize trigger optimization and validation first, not a full create-from-scratch pass.

projection_strategy_explicit.check: Keeps platform differences visible while still treating the canonical description as the primary trigger surface.

validation_executed.check: Uses or references trigger eval prompts and structural validation after description changes.

trigger_work_separated.check: Records description-focused changes separately from body changes and avoids broad churn without evidence.

next_step_explicit.check: Calls out whether more behavior evaluation is needed after the trigger-focused pass.

cases.name: Audit cross-platform readiness of an existing skill library
cases.description: Tests Audit/Governance with cross-platform reasoning. The skill should identify duplicates, stale projections, readiness gaps, and hidden cross-package runtime dependencies, then recommend the next lifecycle step instead of immediately rewriting everything.
cases.prompt: Audit our skill library for cross-platform readiness. I care about duplicate intent, stale or missing Codex and Claude projections, context bloat, and skills that claim to be portable but only work because another skill package's private scripts happen to be present in the same repo. Prioritize the findings and tell me what should happen next.

stage_routing_correct.check: The route should lead with audit/governance and structural checks rather than authoring a new skill.

projection_strategy_explicit.check: Evaluates readiness in terms of canonical source, projection drift, and target-surface expectations.

package_boundary_respected.check: Flags cross-package private script-path dependencies as portability defects instead of treating them as acceptable shared tooling.

codex_projection_present.check: Checks whether Codex projections exist or are in sync when the audited skill claims Codex support.

claude_projection_filtered.check: Checks whether Claude projections exist or are in sync and whether they contain only runtime-relevant files.

validation_executed.check: Runs or references inventory audit and projection validation commands rather than relying only on intuition.

next_step_explicit.check: Ends with a prioritized lifecycle recommendation such as consolidate, project, re-evaluate, or leave as-is.

cases.name: Create a narrow utility skill that is not itself a meta-skill
cases.description: Tests an ordinary skill-authoring request rather than `skill-lifecycle-manager` refining itself. The target skill is a small utility that normalizes frontmatter keys in skill files while still needing canonical packaging and cross-platform projections.
cases.prompt: Turn our repeated markdown frontmatter cleanup workflow into a reusable skill. It should normalize frontmatter keys across skill files, stay narrow and safe, work in both Codex and Claude Code, keep one canonical source of truth, create the projections, and validate the result before you stop.

stage_routing_correct.check: The route should stay focused on authoring, validation, and projection. It should not overfit to meta-skill work just because the manager is evaluating itself.

canonical_core_created_or_preserved.check: Creates the utility skill as a canonical package with `SKILL.md`, `skill.json`, and `CHANGELOG.md`.

projection_strategy_explicit.check: Still distinguishes shared canonical content from platform-specific projections even for an ordinary utility skill.

package_boundary_respected.check: Keeps the new utility skill self-contained after install rather than assuming another skill package's private tooling is present.

codex_projection_present.check: Produces or validates a Codex projection for the new utility skill when distribution is requested.

claude_projection_filtered.check: Produces or validates a Claude Code projection that excludes repo-only metadata or build artifacts.

validation_executed.check: Runs or explicitly reports canonical and projection validation commands.

next_step_explicit.check: Ends with the next lifecycle step, such as running realistic utility prompts or install smoke testing.

cases.name: Rehabilitate an ordinary content skill with separate body and trigger passes
cases.description: Tests a non-meta revision path where the target is an everyday content skill. The response should improve the skill, validate it, evaluate it against realistic work, and keep trigger tuning separate from body fixes.
cases.prompt: Our `release-notes-helper` skill drafts changelog summaries, but users say it misses edge cases and sometimes triggers too broadly on general docs work. Update the skill, validate it, compare the revised version against a meaningful baseline if that helps, and keep description tuning separate from body fixes.

stage_routing_correct.check: The route should focus on revision, validation, evaluation, and a separate trigger pass rather than restarting with a brand-new skill or a full ecosystem survey.

canonical_core_created_or_preserved.check: Treats the existing `release-notes-helper` canonical package as the thing being revised and preserved.

validation_executed.check: Runs or names the relevant validation commands before or alongside evaluation.

trigger_work_separated.check: Keeps the broadness complaint in a distinct trigger-optimization pass instead of mixing description changes into every other edit.

next_step_explicit.check: Ends with a concrete next step such as rerunning realistic release-note prompts or shipping the tightened description.

cases.name: Stop at delegated research checkpoint before authoring
cases.description: Tests the protocol fix for delegated Discover work. A deep-research request that delegates to `skill-researcher` should stop after candidate inventory, ask for confirmation, and avoid drafting the target skill package yet.
cases.prompt: Before we create a new `git worktree` workflow skill, do a deep ecosystem survey first. Use whatever official and community sources you need, but stop after you compile the candidate set so I can approve it before any collection, synthesis, or drafting begins.

stage_routing_correct.check: The route should stay in Discover for this first pass rather than jumping ahead to Create/Update.

delegated_research_protocol_respected.check: Makes the delegated `skill-researcher` workflow explicit and preserves its required pause semantics.

candidate_pause_respected.check: Produces a candidate inventory and clearly asks the user to confirm or adjust it before deeper analysis continues.

no_authoring_before_confirmation.check: Does not begin drafting the target skill package or present authoring artifacts as though confirmation had already happened.

next_step_explicit.check: Ends with the concrete next step of user confirmation of the candidate set.

cases.name: Broad new documentation skill must route through Discover before authoring
cases.description: Tests the regression where a new project-generic, domain-generic skill is broad enough that external comparison and overlap analysis should happen before any package drafting. Because the prompt does not choose Quick, Standard, or Deep, the first pass should stop at the discovery-depth gate.
cases.prompt: We need a project-generic, domain-generic skill that can write repository documentation across many kinds of repos. Use skill-lifecycle-manager and do the right lifecycle stages in the right order.

stage_routing_correct.check: The route should begin with Discover rather than jumping straight to Create/Update or Validate, because the target skill is broad and overlap-prone.

delegated_research_protocol_respected.check: The response should make the delegated `skill-researcher` path or equivalent Discover-first ecosystem handoff explicit instead of treating nearby local skills as sufficient by default.

depth_gate_respected.check: Because broad discovery is required and the prompt does not choose Quick, Standard, or Deep, the response should stop to resolve discovery depth before broad search begins.

no_authoring_before_confirmation.check: The response should not start drafting target-skill files or run validation on target-skill artifacts yet.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should end with the next step of choosing discovery depth, then producing a candidate inventory before any fusion or authoring.

cases.name: Broad new skill with bounded sources should stay narrow instead of widening discovery
cases.description: Tests the bounded-scope exception. Even though the target skill is broad, the user has already constrained the source set, so the response should keep discovery narrow instead of forcing a wider ecosystem survey or a separate depth-choice pause.
cases.prompt: Create a project-generic, domain-generic documentation skill, but keep discovery constrained to our local `readme-craftsman` plus the official OpenAI and Anthropic skill examples. Do not widen the search beyond those sources. After that narrow comparison, draft the skill and validate it.

stage_routing_correct.check: The route should still begin with Discover because the target skill is broad, but it should remain a bounded Discover -> Create/Update -> Validate flow rather than an open-ended survey.

bounded_scope_respected.check: The response should explicitly preserve the user-supplied local plus official source set and avoid widening the search.

depth_gate_respected.check: Because the discovery scope is intentionally bounded, the response should not stop to ask for Quick, Standard, or Deep before proceeding.

canonical_core_created_or_preserved.check: After the bounded comparison, the response should treat the canonical target skill package as the artifact to create.

validation_executed.check: The flow should still include normal structural validation once authoring is complete.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should either complete the bounded route or close with the next concrete step after validation.

cases.name: Specified discovery depth should not trigger a second depth gate
cases.description: Tests the happy path for the new gate. A broad new-skill request that already specifies Standard depth should proceed through Discover and pause at candidate inventory, not stop early to ask for discovery depth again.
cases.prompt: Before drafting a new project-generic documentation skill, do a Standard ecosystem survey first and stop after candidate inventory so I can confirm the set before any deeper analysis or drafting.

stage_routing_correct.check: The route should stay in Discover for this pass because the user explicitly wants the candidate inventory before drafting.

delegated_research_protocol_respected.check: The response should make the delegated `skill-researcher` or equivalent ecosystem-discovery workflow explicit.

depth_gate_respected.check: Because the prompt already specifies Standard depth, the response should not ask for Quick, Standard, or Deep again.

candidate_pause_respected.check: The response should stop at candidate inventory and ask for confirmation before deeper analysis or authoring.

no_authoring_before_confirmation.check: The response should not start drafting the target skill package yet.

next_step_explicit.check: The answer should end with the next step of candidate confirmation, not a second depth-choice question.

cases.name: Fix package-boundary defects before projection or install
cases.description: Tests the install/publish boundary. If a skill only works because it shells out to another skill package's private scripts, the response should treat that as a packaging defect, localize or otherwise ship the required runtime tooling, then validate before projection or install continues.
cases.prompt: Install `agent-lifecycle-manager` for both Codex and Claude Code, but first make sure it is actually portable. During review you discover that its validation and audit workflow only works because the package shells out to private scripts from another installable skill package instead of carrying or explicitly shipping the runtime helpers it needs. Use skill-lifecycle-manager and do the right lifecycle stages in the right order before you project or install anything.

stage_routing_correct.check: The route should repair the package boundary first, then validate, then project or install. It should not jump straight to install.

canonical_core_created_or_preserved.check: Treats the canonical `agent-lifecycle-manager` package as the source of truth and updates that package rather than patching only projected runtime copies.

package_boundary_respected.check: Treats the cross-skill private script-path dependency as a packaging defect and moves or duplicates the required runtime tooling into the installable package or an explicitly shipped distribution unit.

codex_projection_present.check: Projects or validates the repaired package for Codex only after the boundary fix and validation pass.

claude_projection_filtered.check: Projects or validates the repaired package for Claude Code only after the boundary fix and keeps runtime surfaces free of repo-only metadata.

validation_executed.check: Runs or explicitly reports structural and projection validation after the package-boundary repair.

next_step_explicit.check: Ends with the concrete install or smoke-test step that follows the repaired, validated package.

scoring.notes: A case passes if it scores >= 75% of the possible points for the assertions that apply to that case. The suite passes if every case passes individually.

runner_instructions.setup: Install the skill on the target surface(s) or ensure the canonical package is discoverable. If testing projection behavior, prepare writable `.agents/skills/` and `.claude/skills/` surfaces or use a temporary output root.
runner_instructions.execution: Run each prompt in a session where the skill is available. When the case involves projections, capture the generated filesystem artifacts as part of the evidence.
runner_instructions.evaluation: Score each assertion as pass, partial, or fail. For projection assertions, inspect both the canonical package and the generated Codex/Claude directories.
runner_instructions.environment: Cases should be run with filesystem write access. Network access is optional unless Discover is explicitly exercised.
